guideline_references: Therapeutic Guidelines: Rheumatology (acute hot joint/reactive arthritis)
RANZCO guidance on the acute red eye/uveitis
intentional_test_features: Red herrings: a hot ankle with dysuria and red eyes mimics disseminated gonococcal infection and septic arthritis.
Important-not-to-miss: septic/gonococcal arthritis (hot joint → aspirate) and acute anterior uveitis (sight-threatening) must be screened.
Anchoring: the classic reactive triad should not close off a septic/gonococcal joint or an STI needing screening.
Commission trap: IM ketorolac does not translate to a remote injection; anti-inflammatories must not substitute for joint assessment/aspiration.
Telehealth limits: joint examination/aspiration, slit-lamp eye assessment and urinalysis/STI swabs cannot be done over chat.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date)
